# hextimator

> Generate perceptually uniform color palettes with light/dark themes and WCAG contrast guarantees from a single brand color. Outputs CSS variables, Tailwind tokens, SCSS variables, JSON, or plain objects. Designed for runtime use in multi-tenant apps.

## Install

```bash
npm install hextimator
```

## Quick start

```typescript
import { hextimate } from "hextimator";

// Minimal: one color in, full theme out
const theme = hextimate("#6A5ACD").format();
// → { light: { accent: "#6a5acd", "accent-strong": "...", ... }, dark: { ... } }

// With format options — `as: "css"` returns a ready-to-paste stylesheet string
const css = hextimate("#6A5ACD").format({ as: "css", colors: "oklch" });
// → ":root { --accent: oklch(...); ... }\n@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) { :root { ... } }"
```

## API

### `hextimate(color)`

Creates a palette builder from any color input.

- `color`: `string` (hex, CSS function), `[r, g, b]` tuple, `number` (0xFF6666), or a `Color` object
- Returns: `HextimatePaletteBuilder` (chainable)
- Throws: `HextimateError` on invalid input

Tune how the palette is built with `.style(partial)` (`HextimateStyleOptions`, see table below). Presets may include a `style` field; later `.style()` calls merge on top.

### Builder methods (all return `this`, chainable)

| Method | Description |
|---|---|
| `.addRole(name, color)` | Add a new color role with DEFAULT, strong, weak, foreground variants |
| `.addVariant(name, placement)` | Add a lightness step to every role. `placement`: `{ from: "strong" }`, `{ from: "weak" }`, or `{ between: ["DEFAULT", "weak"] }` |
| `.addToken(name, value)` | Add a standalone derived token. `value`: a color, `{ from: "role.variant", emphasis?, lightness?, chroma?, hue? }`, or `{ light: ..., dark: ... }` for per-theme values. `emphasis` is theme-aware (positive = more contrast, negative = softer) |
| `.preset(preset)` | Apply a preset (chainable -- call multiple times to layer style + framework presets). Preset format defaults are overridden by `.format()` options |
| `.style(partial)` | Merge style options into the builder, regenerate base palettes, and replay recorded operations |
| `.simulate(cvdType, severity?)` | Simulate color vision deficiency. Types: `protanopia`, `deuteranopia`, `tritanopia`, `achromatopsia` |
| `.adaptFor(cvdType, severity?)` | Optimize palette for a CVD type (use instead of simulate for actual accessibility) |
| `.fork(color?)` | Clone builder with optional new accent color. Chain `.style()` on the fork to change style options. Original unchanged |
| `.format(options?)` | Output the palette (see format options) |

### `.format(options?)` → `{ light: FormatResult, dark: FormatResult }` or `string`

| Option | Values | Default |
|---|---|---|
| `as` | `"object"`, `"css"`, `"tailwind"`, `"tailwind-css"`, `"scss"`, `"json"` | `"object"` |
| `colors` | `"hex"`, `"rgb"`, `"rgb-raw"`, `"hsl"`, `"hsl-raw"`, `"oklch"`, `"oklch-raw"`, `"p3"`, `"p3-raw"` | `"hex"` |
| `roleNames` | `Record<string, string>` — rename roles in output keys | built-in names |
| `variantNames` | `Record<string, string>` — rename variant suffixes | built-in names |
| `separator` | `string` — between role and variant in keys | `"-"` |
| `darkMode` | `"media"`, `"class"`, `"data-attribute"`, `false` (stylesheet outputs only) | `"media"` |
| `selector` | `string` — root selector for `as: "css"` | `":root"` |
| `excludeRoles` | `string[]` — role keys to omit entirely from output (by internal name, before `roleNames`) | `[]` |
| `excludeVariants` | `string[]` — variant keys to omit from every role's output (by internal name, before `variantNames`) | `[]` |

Output shape depends on `as`:
- `"object"` → `{ light, dark }` flat maps: `{ accent: "#...", "accent-strong": "#..." }`
- `"css"` → `string` — ready-to-paste CSS stylesheet with `:root {}` and a dark-mode wrapper
- `"tailwind"` → `{ light, dark }` nested maps: `{ accent: { DEFAULT: "#...", strong: "#..." } }`
- `"tailwind-css"` → `string` — Tailwind v4 stylesheet: single `@theme { --color-accent: #...; }` block plus dark overrides
- `"scss"` → `{ light, dark }` flat maps: `{ "$accent": "#...", "$accent-strong": "#..." }`
- `"json"` → `{ light, dark }` JSON strings

Pick the dark wrapper with `darkMode`: `"media"` (default) wraps dark tokens in `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)`; `"class"` uses `.dark`; `"data-attribute"` uses `[data-theme="dark"]`; `false` omits dark entirely. For `as: "css"`, change the root selector with `selector` (e.g. `selector: "[data-theme]"`).

### Style options (`HextimateStyleOptions`)

| Option | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `baseColor` | color input | auto-derived | Override base/neutral baseline color |
| `baseHueShift` | degrees | `0` | Rotate base hue relative to accent. Ignored if `baseColor` set |
| `invertDarkModeBaseAccent` | boolean | `false` | Swap base/accent hues in dark mode. Useful when the accent should become the background in dark mode. Requires `baseColor` |
| `semanticColors` | `{ positive?, negative?, warning? }` | auto | Override semantic colors (green/red/amber) |
| `semanticColorRanges` | `{ positive?: [start,end], ... }` | greens, reds, ambers | Hue ranges for semantic detection |
| `baseMaxChroma` | number | `0.01` | Max chroma for base colors |
| `foregroundMaxChroma` | number | `0.01` | Max chroma for foreground colors |
| `light` | `{ lightness?, maxChroma? }` | `{ lightness: 0.7 }` | Light theme adjustments |
| `dark` | `{ lightness?, maxChroma? }` | `{ lightness: 0.6 }` | Dark theme adjustments |
| `minContrastRatio` | `"AAA"`, `"AA"`, or number | `"AAA"` | WCAG contrast target (AAA=7, AA=4.5) |
| `hueShift` | degrees | `0` | Per-variant hue rotation |

### Default palette structure

Every palette includes 5 roles x 4 variants + 2 brand tokens = 22 tokens:

- Roles: `base`, `accent`, `positive`, `negative`, `warning`
- Variants: `DEFAULT` (no suffix), `strong`, `weak`, `foreground`
- Brand tokens: `brand-exact` (the exact input color, unmodified), `brand-exact-foreground` (best-contrast foreground for that color)

Token keys: `accent`, `accent-strong`, `accent-weak`, `accent-foreground`, `base`, `base-strong`, ..., `brand-exact`, `brand-exact-foreground`

### Presets

Presets are predefined configurations -- for a specific framework or a particular style. They can be chained by calling `.preset()` multiple times. Applied sequentially: later presets override earlier ones for conflicting keys. Call `.style()` after `.preset()` to override overlapping style keys.

**Available presets:**

| Preset | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `shadcn` | Framework | shadcn/ui tokens: --background, --primary, --destructive, --ring, etc. `as: "css"`, `colors: "oklch"` |
| `mui` | Framework | MUI theme: primary/secondary/error with main/light/dark/contrastText. `as: "object"`, `colors: "hex"` |
| `muted` | Style | Desaturated, restrained. Low chroma across the board |
| `vibrant` | Style | High saturation, complementary tinted neutrals, slight hue shift |
| `tinted` | Style | Neutrals pick up the accent hue for a cohesive feel |

```typescript
import { hextimate, presets } from "hextimator";

// Framework preset
const theme = hextimate("#6366F1")
  .preset(presets.shadcn)
  .format();

// Style + framework preset
const theme = hextimate("#6366F1")
  .preset(presets.muted)
  .preset(presets.shadcn)
  .format();

// Override preset defaults:
const theme = hextimate("#6366F1")
  .preset(presets.muted)
  .preset(presets.shadcn)
  .format({ colors: "hsl-raw" });
```

**Chaining merge rules:**
- Preset `style` / `.style()`: nested keys `light`, `dark`, `semanticColors`, `semanticColorRanges` shallow-merge per key; other keys overwrite (later wins)
- Roles, variants, tokens: concatenated (additive)
- Format options: deep-merged (roleNames/variantNames merged individually)

### Creating presets

A preset is a plain `HextimatePreset` object with optional fields for `style`, `roles`, `variants`, `tokens`, and `format`. It configures the builder the same way chained methods would, but as a reusable data object.

```typescript
import type { HextimatePreset } from "hextimator";
```

**Interface:**

```typescript
interface HextimatePreset {
  style?: HextimateStyleOptions;  // contrast, lightness, chroma, hue shifts
  roles?: Array<{ name: string; color: ColorInput | DerivedToken }>;
  variants?: Array<{ name: string; placement: VariantPlacement }>;
  tokens?: Array<{ name: string; value: TokenValue }>;
  format?: HextimateFormatOptions;  // default format options, overridable in .format()
}
```

**Token values** can be:
- A raw color: `"#FF6600"`, `[255, 102, 102]`, etc.
- A derived reference: `{ from: "role.variant", emphasis?, lightness?, chroma?, hue? }`
- Per-theme: `{ light: DerivedToken | ColorInput, dark: DerivedToken | ColorInput }`

**`from` syntax**: `"role"` resolves to the DEFAULT variant, `"role.variant"` to a specific one. Valid built-in references: `base`, `base.strong`, `base.weak`, `base.foreground`, `accent`, `accent.strong`, etc. Custom roles/tokens added earlier in the preset can also be referenced.

**`emphasis`** is theme-aware contrast adjustment. Positive values push the color *away* from the background (more contrast), negative values pull it *toward* the background (softer). The direction flips automatically between light and dark themes, so `emphasis: 0.12` always means "slightly more visible than the source" regardless of theme. Use this instead of per-theme `lightness` splits when you just want "a bit more/less contrast."

**`roleNames` / `variantNames`** in format options remap output keys. This is how you match another framework's naming. For example, `roleNames: { base: "background", accent: "primary" }` turns `base-strong` into `background-strong` and `accent` into `primary` in the output. `variantNames: { DEFAULT: "main", foreground: "contrastText" }` turns `accent` (DEFAULT) into `primary-main` and `accent-foreground` into `primary-contrastText`.

**Role colors** can be derived from other roles: `{ from: "accent", hue: 180 }` creates a role whose hue is rotated 180 degrees from the accent.

**Example - custom preset for a design system with "surface", "brand", and "danger" tokens:**

```typescript
const myPreset: HextimatePreset = {
  roles: [
    // Add an "info" role not in the default palette
    { name: "info", color: "#0288d1" },
    // Derive a secondary role by rotating the accent hue
    { name: "secondary", color: { from: "accent", hue: 180 } },
  ],
  tokens: [
    // Surface tokens derived from base
    { name: "surface", value: { from: "base" } },
    { name: "surface-raised", value: { from: "base", emphasis: -0.025 } },
    { name: "surface-overlay", value: { from: "base", emphasis: -0.05 } },

    // Text with varying prominence
    { name: "text-primary", value: { from: "base.foreground" } },
    { name: "text-secondary", value: { from: "base.foreground", emphasis: -0.2 } },
    { name: "text-disabled", value: { from: "base.foreground", emphasis: -0.4 } },

    // Border that adjusts direction per theme
    { name: "border", value: { from: "base", emphasis: 0.12 } },

    // Per-theme token when emphasis alone isn't enough
    {
      name: "divider",
      value: {
        light: { from: "base.weak", lightness: -0.08 },
        dark: { from: "base.weak", lightness: +0.08 },
      },
    },
  ],
  format: {
    as: "css",
    colors: "hex",
    roleNames: {
      base: "surface",
      accent: "brand",
      negative: "danger",
      positive: "success",
    },
    variantNames: {
      DEFAULT: "",        // no suffix for the base variant
      strong: "bold",
      weak: "subtle",
      foreground: "on",   // e.g. --brand-on
    },
    separator: "-",
  },
};
```

Usage:

```typescript
import { hextimate } from "hextimator";

const theme = hextimate("#6366F1").preset(myPreset).format();
// .format() options override preset defaults:
const asScss = hextimate("#6366F1").preset(myPreset).format({ as: "scss" });
```

**How to build a preset for an existing app's theme:**

1. List the app's theme tokens (e.g. from its CSS variables or theme config)
2. Map each token to a hextimator source: which built-in role/variant is it closest to?
3. Use `roleNames`/`variantNames` to rename the 20 built-in tokens to match the app's naming
4. Use `tokens` for any extra tokens not covered by the built-in 5x4 grid, deriving them with `from` + `emphasis`/`lightness`/`chroma`/`hue` offsets
5. Use `roles` if the app has color categories beyond base/accent/positive/negative/warning
6. Set `format` defaults to match what the app expects (CSS vars, object, etc.)

### Standalone tokens with per-theme values

```typescript
hextimate("#6366F1")
  .addToken("border", {
    light: { from: "base.weak", lightness: -0.08 },
    dark: { from: "base.weak", lightness: +0.08 },
  })
  .addToken("ring", { from: "accent" })
  .format({ as: "css" });
```

### Utility exports

```typescript
import { parseColor, convertColor } from "hextimator";

const color = parseColor("rgb(255, 102, 102)");  // → Color object (sRGB)
const oklch = convertColor(color, "oklch");       // → OKLCH object
```

Also exported: `daltonizeColor(color, cvdType, severity?)`, `simulateColor(color, cvdType, severity?)`.

### Color input formats

All of these are valid:

```typescript
hextimate("#FF6666");            // hex string
hextimate("FF6666");             // hex without #
hextimate("rgb(255, 102, 102)"); // CSS function
hextimate("hsl(0, 100%, 70%)"); // CSS HSL
hextimate([255, 102, 102]);      // RGB tuple
hextimate(0xff6666);             // numeric hex
```

Alpha is always ignored (forced to 1) to preserve contrast guarantees.

## CLI

```bash
npx hextimator <color> [options]
# or the shorter alias:
npx hextimate <color> [options]
```

Both commands are identical. Use whichever you prefer.

| Flag | Description | Default |
|---|---|---|
| `-f, --format` | `css`, `object`, `tailwind`, `tailwind-css`, `scss`, `json` | `css` |
| `-c, --colors` | `hex`, `rgb`, `rgb-raw`, `hsl`, `hsl-raw`, `oklch`, `oklch-raw`, `p3`, `p3-raw` | `hex` |
| `-t, --theme` | `light`, `dark`, `both` (ignored for `css`/`tailwind-css` — both always combine into one string) | `both` |
| `-p, --preset` | Apply preset (repeatable, applied in order): `shadcn`, `mui`, `muted`, `vibrant`, `tinted` | — |
| `--separator` | Token separator between role and variant | `-` |
| `--dark-mode` | `media`, `class`, `data-attribute`, `false` (stylesheet outputs only) | `media` |
| `--selector` | Root selector for `--format css` | `:root` |
| `--exclude-role` | Omit a role from output by internal name (repeatable) | — |
| `--exclude-variant` | Omit a variant from output by internal name (repeatable) | — |
| `--base-color` | Override base color | — |
| `--base-hue-shift` | Degrees | `0` |
| `--hue-shift` | Per-variant hue shift | `0` |
| `--base-max-chroma` | Max chroma for base | `0.01` |
| `--fg-max-chroma` | Max chroma for foreground | `0.01` |
| `--light-lightness` | Light theme lightness 0-1 | `0.7` |
| `--light-max-chroma` | Light theme max chroma | — |
| `--dark-lightness` | Dark theme lightness 0-1 | `0.6` |
| `--dark-max-chroma` | Dark theme max chroma | — |
| `--min-contrast` | `AAA`, `AA`, or number | `AAA` |
| `--invert-dark` | Swap base/accent hues in dark mode (requires --base-color) | — |
| `--positive` | Override positive/success color | auto |
| `--negative` | Override negative/error color | auto |
| `--warning` | Override warning color | auto |
| `--simulate` | Simulate CVD: `protanopia`, `deuteranopia`, `tritanopia`, `achromatopsia` | — |
| `--adapt` | Adapt palette for CVD type | — |
| `--cvd-severity` | CVD severity 0-1 | `1` |
| `--role <name>=<color>` | Add custom role (repeatable) | — |
| `--variant <spec>` | Add variant: `name:from:edge` or `name:between:a,b` (repeatable) | — |
| `-o, --output <path>` | Write to file instead of stdout | stdout |

```bash
# Generate CSS palette
npx hextimator "#6A5ACD" --format css --colors oklch

# shadcn/ui preset
npx hextimator "#6366F1" --preset shadcn

# Chained presets: muted style + shadcn framework
npx hextimator "#6366F1" --preset muted --preset shadcn

# Invert base/accent in dark mode
npx hextimator "#6A5ACD" --base-color "#FEBA5D" --invert-dark

# Custom roles and variants
npx hextimator "#22aa44" --role cta=#ee2244 --variant hover:from:strong -o theme.css

# Simulate color blindness
npx hextimator "#ff6600" --simulate deuteranopia

# Adapt for color blindness
npx hextimator "#ff6600" --adapt deuteranopia --cvd-severity 0.8
```

## React

```typescript
import { useHextimator } from "hextimator/react";

function App() {
  useHextimator("#6A5ACD");
  return <div className="bg-accent text-accent-foreground">Themed!</div>;
}
```

Dark mode strategies:
- `{ type: "media" }` (default) -- uses `prefers-color-scheme` media query
- `{ type: "class", className?: string }` -- toggles on a CSS class (default: `"dark"`)
- `{ type: "data", attribute?: string }` -- toggles on a data attribute (default: `"data-theme"`)
- `{ type: "media-or-class", className?: string }` -- media query with class override
- `false` -- disable dark mode entirely

Hook options:

| Option | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `style` | `HextimateStyleOptions` | Palette style options |
| `presets` | `HextimatePreset[]` | Presets to apply in order (style + framework) |
| `format` | `Omit<HextimateFormatOptions, "as">` | Format options (output is always CSS vars) |
| `configure` | `(builder: HextimatePaletteBuilder) => void` | Customize the builder (add roles, tokens, etc.) |
| `darkMode` | `DarkModeStrategy` | Dark mode strategy (see above) |
| `cssPrefix` | `string` | Prefix for all CSS variable names |
| `target` | `RefObject<HTMLElement>` | Inject variables into a specific element instead of `:root` |

### Provider

For app-wide theming with dynamic color changes:

```typescript
import { HextimatorProvider, useHextimatorTheme } from "hextimator/react";

<HextimatorProvider
  defaultColor="#6A5ACD"            // string OR { light: "#...", dark: "#..." }
  defaultMode="system"              // "light" | "dark" | "system"
  onColorChange={(next) => persist(next)}     // fires on every color change
  onModePreferenceChange={(mode) => persist(mode)}
  style={styleOpts}
  presets={[presets.muted, presets.shadcn]}
  format={formatOpts}
  darkMode={{ type: "class" }}
  cssPrefix="app"
  target={containerRef}
>
  <App />
</HextimatorProvider>
```

**Per-mode colors:** pass `defaultColor={{ light: "#FF6600", dark: "#0088FF" }}` to use a different brand color per mode. Both palettes are built and stitched in CSS, so `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)` always serves the dark palette correctly. When `light === dark` (default for a string), only one palette is built.

**Persistence:** `onColorChange` and `onModePreferenceChange` always fire on user-driven updates. Wire them to localStorage / cookies / etc. Restore by reading once on mount and passing the result to `defaultColor` / `defaultMode`. For forced overrides (URL-driven, server push), remount with `key={color}`.

**FOUC:** the provider injects CSS in `useEffect` (after first paint). To avoid flash on SPAs, read storage synchronously in a lazy initializer so `defaultColor` is already correct on the first render. For SSR (Next.js, Remix), persist in a cookie and render `<HextimatorStyle>` server-side in the layout `<head>`.

`useHextimatorTheme()` returns:

| Property | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `color` | `string` | Color active for the resolved mode (= `lightColor` in light, `darkColor` in dark) |
| `setColor` | `(color: string) => void` | Sets BOTH `lightColor` and `darkColor` (one-color-in, whole-theme-out) |
| `lightColor` | `string` | Color used to generate the light-mode palette |
| `setLightColor` | `(color: string) => void` | Update only the light-mode color |
| `darkColor` | `string` | Color used to generate the dark-mode palette |
| `setDarkColor` | `(color: string) => void` | Update only the dark-mode color |
| `palette` | `HextimateResult` | The generated `{ light, dark }` palette (light from `lightColor`, dark from `darkColor`) |
| `mode` | `"light" \| "dark"` | Resolved color mode (accounts for OS preference) |
| `modePreference` | `"light" \| "dark" \| "system"` | Raw preference before resolution |
| `setMode` | `(mode: ModePreference) => void` | Change mode preference |
| `style` | `HextimateStyleOptions \| undefined` | Current style options |
| `setStyle` | `(opts) => void` | Update style options |
| `presets` | `HextimatePreset[] \| undefined` | Current presets |
| `setPresets` | `(presets) => void` | Update presets (triggers re-generation) |
| `configure` | `((builder) => void) \| undefined` | Current builder configure function |
| `setConfigure` | `(fn) => void` | Update the configure callback |

### `HextimatorStyle`

Declarative alternative to `useHextimator`. Renders a `<style>` element with CSS variables directly in the tree -- works during SSR/RSC (no `useEffect`). Optional `selector` for per-subtree scoping via CSS cascade.

```tsx
import { HextimatorStyle } from "hextimator/react";

// Global theme (SSR-safe)
<HextimatorStyle color="#ff6600" darkMode={{ type: "class" }} />

// Scoped to a specific subtree
<div className="card-a">
  <HextimatorStyle color="#ff0066" selector=".card-a" />
  ...
</div>
```

Props: same as `useHextimator` options, plus `selector?: string` (defaults to `:root`).

### `HextimatorScope`

Subtree theming with automatic shape inheritance. Wraps children in a scoped `<div>` with its own `<style>`. When nested inside a `HextimatorProvider` or another `HextimatorScope`, it inherits all custom roles, variants, tokens, and presets from the parent via `builder.fork()` -- you only need to pass the new color.

```tsx
import { HextimatorProvider, HextimatorScope } from "hextimator/react";

<HextimatorProvider
  defaultColor="#ff6600"
  presets={[presets.shadcn]}
  darkMode={{ type: "class" }}
  configure={(b) => b.addRole("cta", "#ff0066")}
>
  <App>
    {/* Inherits shadcn preset + cta role, just swaps color */}
    <HextimatorScope defaultColor="#0066ff" darkMode={{ type: "class" }}>
      <Card />
    </HextimatorScope>
  </App>
</HextimatorProvider>
```

Props: `defaultColor` (`string \| { light, dark }`), `isolated?`, `style?`, `presets?`, `format?`, `configure?`, `darkMode?`, `cssPrefix?`, `className?`, `wrapperStyle?` (inline styles for the scope element).

## `fallback.css`

Pre-JS placeholder: neutral values for the same variables as default `hextimate()` output. Use in client-rendered apps so the first paint has `--accent`, `--base`, etc. before the bundle runs. Runtime theme CSS then replaces them.

```css
@import "hextimator/fallback.css";
@import "hextimator/tailwind.css";  /* if using Tailwind */
```

Does not cover preset-only token names (e.g. shadcn's `--ring`). Use the CLI to generate a static file for those.

## Tailwind CSS v4

```css
@import "tailwindcss";
@import "hextimator/tailwind.css";
```

This registers all built-in tokens as Tailwind utilities (`bg-accent`, `text-base-foreground`, etc.).

## Common recipes

### Inverted dark mode (accent as background)

```typescript
const theme = hextimate("#52FE8C")
  .style({
    baseColor: "#FEBA5D",
    invertDarkModeBaseAccent: true,
  })
  .format({ as: "css" });
// Dark mode uses the accent hue as background, base hue as accent
```

### shadcn/ui theme

```typescript
import { hextimate, presets } from "hextimator";

hextimate("#6366F1")
  .preset(presets.shadcn)
  .format();
// Generates all shadcn/ui tokens with proper naming
```

### Multi-tenant with `.fork()`

```typescript
const base = hextimate("#52FE8C")
  .style({ baseColor: "#FEBA5D" })
  .addRole("banner", "#ff006e")
  .addVariant("hover", { from: "strong" });

const tenantA = base.fork("#ff6677").format({ as: "css" });
const tenantB = base.fork("#3a86ff").format({ as: "css" });
```

### Color vision deficiency

```typescript
const normalTheme = hextimate("#ff6600");
const adaptedTheme = normalTheme.fork().adaptFor("deuteranopia");
const preview = normalTheme.fork().simulate("deuteranopia");
```

## Optional

- [Extending the palette](https://github.com/fgrgic/hextimator/blob/main/packages/hextimator/docs/extending-the-palette.md): addRole, addVariant, addToken in depth
- [Customization reference](https://github.com/fgrgic/hextimator/blob/main/packages/hextimator/docs/customization.md): all style and format options
- [Multiple themes](https://github.com/fgrgic/hextimator/blob/main/packages/hextimator/docs/multiple-themes.md): .fork() and dynamic theming
- [Color vision deficiency](https://github.com/fgrgic/hextimator/blob/main/packages/hextimator/docs/color-vision-deficiency.md): simulate and adaptFor
- [React docs](https://github.com/fgrgic/hextimator/blob/main/packages/hextimator/docs/react.md): hook, provider, dark mode strategies
- [Tailwind CSS v4](https://github.com/fgrgic/hextimator/blob/main/packages/hextimator/docs/tailwind.md): setup and custom tokens
- [Real-world examples](https://github.com/fgrgic/hextimator/blob/main/packages/hextimator/docs/examples.md): shadcn/ui, Stripe, Slack configs
